title: CEOs of the future — drugs, tech-literacy, and the slow slide
html:
TL;DR

  • Two questions Yahir wants to ask someone (likely a founder/exec): "Do you think CEOs of the future will be at a disadvantage if they are not engineers or don't have an understanding of technology?" and "What is one thing you recommend we do in college?"
  • Sandwiched between is a self-aware observation about drug use creep: "You don't know what you have become until you have become it. Drugs, doesn't happen, then it starts and u forgot how it started."
  • Closes with self-directive: "Yeah you should probably stop drinking and smoking. You kind of new past then" — direct second-person self-talk.
  • Earliest entry in the drugs thesis thread that culminates in 2021 12 05 hey cameron yg thesis on drugs (the Mario-Kart banana metaphor).
  • The CEO-tech-literacy question is the same instinct that years later powers Yahir building rankett solo with anthropic claude and windsurf ide — he made himself the engineer.

Key quotes

"Do you think that CEO's of the future will be at a disadvantage if they are not engineers or don't have an understanding of technology?"

"You don't know what you have become until you have become it. Drugs, doesn't happened, then it starts and u forgot how it started"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Marks the start of his explicit drug-use self-monitoring period (Nov 2021), feeding into 2021 12 05 hey cameron yg thesis on drugs a month later.
  • The CEO-as-engineer thesis is a 2021 belief that he later operationalized — he became the engineer.
  • Foreshadows his cofounder analysis of Cam in 2025 08 29 irl lore tab 7 — Cam can't develop, hence the strain.

Open questions

  • Who was he going to ask the CEO question to? Faculty? A speaker?
  • What triggered the drug self-talk in November 2021 specifically?

Wikilinks
